name: ce-meet
currentState: ce-meet is a Rust library plus thin CLI implementing only the SIGNALING plane of a Meet-like app on the CE mesh; the media plane (audio/video) is explicitly out of scope (browser WebRTC). The signaling logic is genuinely well-built and pure. proto.rs defines a sequence-numbered SignalEnvelope wrapping a Signal enum (Join/Leave/Keepalive/Offer/Answer/IceCandidate/IceEnd). room.rs implements a clean LWW-per-member CRDT roster with convergence, snapshot/restore, merge, and liveness pruning. order.rs is a per-peer reorder buffer (OrderedInbox/SignalRouter) giving in-order de-duplicated SDP/ICE delivery with a bounded window. caps.rs plus admit.rs implement capability-gated admission over ce-cap with a MAC'd, identity-bound ResumeToken and constant-time comparison. turn.rs defines IceServer/TurnCredential config types with channel-bound ephemeral TURN-REST credential derivation. client.rs (MeetClient) and main.rs (CLI: create-room/join/signal/host) wire it to the ce-rs SDK; all SDK methods used were verified to exist. What works: the entire signaling/roster/admission/ordering state machine, exercised by ~70 unit/integration/property tests. What is documentation-only/stubbed: the TURN coturn sidecar, the credential-issuance endpoint, the SFU cell, relay discovery wiring (find_service referenced only in docs), and ALL media-control signaling (mute, screen-share, recording, chat, reactions, raise-hand). No unwrap/expect/panic/todo in any non-test path. The CLI is a 1s poll loop, not SSE-driven real-time.
plan: Add in-call media-control signaling: extend Signal with Mute{audio,video}, ScreenShare{active}, and optionally RaiseHand/Reaction/Chat broadcast variants; thread mute/camera/sharing state into Member and surface as new Effect variants. Highest-leverage gap and fits the existing envelope/CRDT model directly.

Add bounds/DoS guards: configurable max-members cap in Room, max-peers cap on SignalRouter.inboxes, and max-payload plus max-SDP/candidate-length checks in from_bytes/Signal validation. Add failure-path tests for each.
Switch the CLI and provide a library helper to drive MeetClient::ingest_ordered from the ce-rs SSE message stream in real time instead of the 1s/500ms poll loop; keep poll as fallback.

Implement host moderation: Kick/RemoveParticipant and EndRoom signals gated by ABILITY_MODERATE/ABILITY_HOST through the Gate, a lobby/waiting-room admission mode, and mute-others. Wire the currently-unused ability constants into enforcement.

Build the first real media tier: wire relay discovery (find_service(SERVICE_TURN), rank by atlas/RTT) into the Admitter so AdmitResp returns live TURN IceServers, and implement the channel-bound TURN credential issuance path (coturn sidecar + REST endpoint). Treat SFU as a later milestone.
Replace both MAC constructions (admit.rs resume_mac, turn.rs derive) with HMAC-SHA256 or keyed blake3, and make turn.rs verify use constant-time comparison like admit.rs ct_eq.

Enforce or remove the sent_at freshness claim: implement a configurable freshness window that discards stale directed signals (and document it), or strip the misleading guarantee from proto.rs/room.rs docs.

Add integration tests with a mock CeClient covering MeetClient.poll/subscribe/request_admission and the host admit-reply loop, plus a CLI e2e test (create-room -> host -> join -> signal). Add admit-loop rate limiting and a flood-resilience test.
Add an examples/ directory (two-party signaling walkthrough) and an architecture/sequence doc; add an explicit Implemented-vs-Planned section to README and turn.rs.
Add property tests for OrderedInbox under extreme/wraparound seqs and for the new bounds (members/peers/payload); add RecordingStarted/Stopped consent signaling if recording is in scope.

featureGaps.feature: Actual media plane (TURN relay server + SFU cell)
featureGaps.notes: Only config TYPES exist (IceServer, TurnCredential). The coturn sidecar, the channel-bound TURN credential issuance endpoint, relay discovery wiring (find_service(meet:turn) is documented in turn.rs but never called), and the SFU cell image are all absent. Symmetric-NAT peers and large rooms cannot connect. Google Meet is fundamentally an SFU.

featureGaps.feature: In-call media-control signaling: mute/unmute, camera on/off, screen-share start/stop
featureGaps.notes: The Signal enum has no Mute, VideoState, or ScreenShare variants. Meet shows live mute/camera/sharing state per participant; ce-meet roster has no such concept.
No recording primitive at all. Even signaling RecordingStarted/Stopped to the roster is missing.
No Chat/Reaction/RaiseHand signal. These fit the existing broadcast envelope model trivially but are unmodeled.
main.rs join/host loops poll ce.messages() on a fixed timer (1000ms/500ms). WebRTC needs sub-second signaling; docs reference SSE and ingest_ordered but the CLI never uses them.

featureGaps.feature: Moderation: kick/remove, end-for-all, lobby/waiting room, mute-others
featureGaps.notes: ABILITY_MODERATE and ABILITY_HOST constants exist but no Kick/EndRoom/MuteParticipant signal or enforcement path uses them. The Admitter only does the join-gate, no eject.

featureGaps.feature: Participant metadata beyond name (role, connection quality, speaking)
featureGaps.notes: Member carries only display_name + presence; no role (host/cohost), connection-quality, or speaking indicator.
Rooms are ephemeral random ids; no calendar integration, human-readable names, or PSTN dial-in.
Identity is authenticated at signaling but DTLS-SRTP fingerprints carried in SDP are not independently verified or surfaced by the app.

robustnessIssues.issue: No upper bound on roster size. Room.members (HashMap) grows unbounded; a peer can publish Join envelopes with many distinct from-NodeIds with no cap, enabling memory-exhaustion DoS. Add a max-members limit.

No upper bound on SignalRouter.inboxes (one OrderedInbox per distinct from). A flood of directed signals from many senders allocates unbounded per-peer buffers; each buffer is window-bounded but the count is not.

No size/sanity validation on SDP or ICE-candidate bodies. Offer.sdp/Answer.sdp/IceCandidate.candidate are arbitrary-length Strings forwarded verbatim; from_bytes has no max-payload guard. A peer can publish multi-megabyte blobs.

sent_at freshness is documented but never enforced. Room::apply and OrderedInbox ignore sent_at entirely (only seq is used), so stale-but-higher-seq directed signals are delivered. Either implement a freshness window or stop documenting the guarantee.

Resume-token and TURN MAC use raw SHA256(secret||fields), not HMAC. NUL-separated and domain-prefixed, mitigating trivial length-extension, but a true keyed MAC (HMAC-SHA256/keyed blake3) is the correct primitive.
turn.rs TurnCredential::verify compares password with a plain == on hex strings, unlike admit.rs which uses constant-time ct_eq; inconsistent and a minor timing side-channel.
to_bytes() swallows serialization errors and silently emits an empty-object body on failure; for these plain types it is infallible, but the fallback masks a bug rather than surfacing it.
No rate limiting on admission requests. The host runs full ce-cap signature verification for every meet:admit message each poll; an attacker can flood admit requests to burn host CPU.

Host reply uses serde_json::to_vec(&resp).unwrap_or_default(); on the impossible error it sends an empty body the joiner cannot parse, surfacing as a generic transport error instead of an explicit denial.

testGaps.area: MeetClient async I/O paths (poll, subscribe, request_admission, send_admit) against a mock CeClient; only pure apply/ingest are unit-tested
CLI commands (create-room, join, signal, host) end-to-end including the host admit/reply round trip
Roster/router memory bounds under a flood of distinct senders (no cap exists, so no test asserts one)
Concurrent host plus multiple joiners admission race / resume-token reuse across overlapping reconnects
Oversized/adversarial SDP and candidate payloads (size, control chars, non-SDP garbage) reaching apply
sent_at freshness behavior (unused field; a property test would expose the doc/behavior mismatch)
OrderedInbox window force-advance under adversarial seq patterns near u64::MAX wraparound
TurnCredential::verify timing side-channel and the plain-equality password compare

docGaps: README/rustdoc are thorough but oversell: turn.rs, the README media-tier table, and lib.rs describe TURN-via-relay, SFU cells, and relay discovery as if integrated when they are config-types-only with no wiring. Add an explicit Implemented-vs-Planned boundary.
No examples/ directory despite CLI categories; the only runnable example is the lib.rs no_run doctest. Add a two-party signaling walkthrough.
No architecture/sequence doc for the join handshake (request_admission -> Admitter -> AdmitResp -> subscribe -> announce_join -> SDP/ICE) which spans 4 files.
No documentation of the security rationale for the non-HMAC SHA256(secret||...) MAC construction.
sent_at is documented as a freshness guarantee in proto.rs and room.rs but is never enforced (actively misleading).
No CHANGELOG and no note that the CLI poll loop is unsuitable for real WebRTC timing.

name: ce-drive
currentState: ce-drive is a Rust workspace (4 crates) reimagining Google Drive on CE primitives, and it is genuinely substantial — ~10k lines of real, non-stub logic with strong tests. ce-drive-core is the heart: tree.rs implements Kleppmann's highly-available move-CRDT (stable NodeId edges, derived paths, cycle-skip via is_ancestor, undo/do/redo log integration, deterministic conflict-rename) as a ce-coord StateMachine; content.rs is an orthogonal NodeId-keyed content map with LWW-by-(mtime,cid) and a capped (32) version history; store.rs does real chunk/dedup/delta-upload/CID-verify over rdev::chunk+delta and ce-rs; drive.rs (single-writer) and sync.rs (multi-writer over ce_coord::Merged) both compose them; share.rs uses ce-cap attenuating capability chains with path-prefix scope, expiry, on-chain revocation and a `..` guard; durability.rs and audit.rs add pin policy and on-chain grant/revoke audit. ce-drive-mount has a complete OS-independent VFS engine (lazy hydration, write-back cache, stable inodes, readdirplus, atomic rename) plus a driverless materialize/push/watch fallback; the Linux fuser adapter is feature-gated and real, while the macOS (FSKit) and Windows (WinFsp/ProjFS) adapters are cfg/feature-gated and only compile-checked on their target OS. ce-drive-wasm is an I/O-free CRDT compiled to wasm for the in-browser node, with golden-wire parity tests against core. What is NOT built: the Drive web app/UI (M3), full-text search, thumbnails/previews, a change-feed/cursor read API, and trash GC / empty-trash / restore-from-trash (only a policy retention field exists). No todo!()/unimplemented!()/panic! in production paths; every unwrap/expect found is in test code (the only non-test .lock().unwrap() is on a never-poisoned Lamport mutex).
plan: Add a streaming/bounded I/O path: make Store::put_bytes/put_file and VFS hydrate/flush operate chunk-by-chunk without buffering the whole file in RAM, plus a configurable max-file-size guard; add a large-file integration test proving no OOM. Top robustness fix; unblocks real-world files.

Implement the trash lifecycle: restore-from-trash (mv out of TRASH), empty-trash, and a GC pass honoring PinPolicy.trash_retention_secs that emits ContentOp::Remove for orphaned CIDs and unpins via durability. Add Drive/SyncedDrive methods + CLI subcommands (`trash`, `restore`, `empty-trash`) + tests.

Add a change-feed/cursor API: `changes_since(cursor) -> (Vec<ChangeEvent>, next_cursor)` over the ts-ordered tree+content op logs, cursor = last-seen Timestamp. Foundation for an efficient web UI, activity feed, and incremental client sync; cheap given the existing total order.

Build the M3 Drive web app skeleton: a `ce-drive serve` axum endpoint (authorize_path-gated via ce-cap, serving listings/blobs/op-feed) plus a minimal React frontend on the wasm CRDT (folder browser, upload/download, share dialog, trash, version history). An MVP closes the biggest product gap.

Enforce name validation in ce-drive-core (reject empty, '/', '..', NUL, overlong names) at mkdir/add_file/mv with clear errors; normalize case/backslashes in the share scope check to prevent bypass. Add failure-path tests.

Make rename truly atomic: replace rm()+mv() overwrite with a single transactional model mutation (or order so the destination is never lost on failure); add concurrency tests for rename-during-write and overwrite-rename.

Optimize hot paths: cache defining_ts on the edge instead of scanning the log; make single-writer apply_remote_content incremental (insert+apply, not full re-fold) or guard the O(n^2); batch the delta get_blob existence probe into one has-chunks call. Add a perf regression test.

Add log compaction/checkpointing to the single-writer Drive (SyncedDrive already has checkpoint()), bounding the CLI state file and replay time; persist via bincode+zstd per project standards instead of pretty JSON for large drives.

Surface concurrent file-content conflict copies: when a remote content op loses LWW but is a genuine concurrent edit, render a visible `*.conflict` sibling (Dropbox-style) rather than burying it in version history only.
Enable the multi-writer two-node mesh test and a wasm-bindgen browser test in CI (build the ce binary in the lane) so convergence and the JS surface are exercised rather than silently skipped.
Add full-text + filename search (M7): index FileContent metadata and text blobs into a queryable index with a `search(query)` API and CLI/web surface.
Refresh docs: rewrite README to reflect the shipped mount + wasm crates, add an architecture doc, add runnable examples/ and a CHANGELOG.

featureGaps.feature: Drive web UI (folder browser, upload/download, share dialog, trash, version history)
featureGaps.notes: M3 is entirely unbuilt. There is no HTTP server, no React/frontend, no `ce-drive serve` endpoint. The wasm CRDT exists but nothing consumes it. This is the single biggest gap vs Google Drive, which is fundamentally a web product.
No search index, no query API, no filename/content search. Google Drive's search is a core surface. Mentioned only as M7 in design; zero code.

No `changes(since_cursor)` API. Drive's Changes API (startPageToken/nextPageToken) powers efficient client sync and activity feeds. Clients must currently re-read whole DriveState. The op logs already carry total-order Timestamps, so a cursor=last-seen-ts feed is achievable but absent.

featureGaps.feature: Trash lifecycle: empty-trash, restore-from-trash, hard-delete GC
featureGaps.notes: rm() moves to TRASH but there is no restore (move back out of TRASH), no empty-trash, and no GC that honors PinPolicy.trash_retention_secs / ContentOp::Remove. trash_retention_secs is dead config. CIDs are never unpinned.

featureGaps.feature: Thumbnails / previews / file metadata (content type, MIME)
featureGaps.notes: FileContent stores cid/size/mode/mtime only — no content-type, no preview/thumbnail generation. Drive shows rich previews.
Blobs are stored as plaintext CIDs; the design's optional E2E envelope (M6) is not implemented. Sharing controls access but content is not encrypted to recipients.
Ability::Comment and FileContent.doc_id exist as reserved hooks but no comment store and no ce-notes embedding (M6).

Tree name-collisions render deterministic *.conflict copies, but concurrent edits to the SAME file id resolve by LWW (older edit retained only in version history, never surfaced as a visible conflict copy the way Drive/Dropbox do).
No per-workspace quota enforcement; durability rent is delegated to ce-pin but not wired. DoS via unbounded files/versions is unchecked.
Sharing is capability-based (no member list by design), but there is no UX/API to enumerate what a workspace has shared beyond the local audit journal, and no recipient-side 'shared with me' view.

robustnessIssues.issue: Whole-file read into memory with no size bound: Store::put_file does std::fs::read(path) and put_bytes takes &[u8]; VFS hydrate() and flush() also buffer the entire file in RAM (OpenFile.buf: Vec<u8>). A multi-GB file OOMs the process; there is no streaming/chunked upload path or max-size guard despite the 'open a 2GiB file, read header pulls one chunk' claim in vfs.rs docs (read() actually hydrates the WHOLE file).

Delta upload does a get_blob existence probe per distinct chunk (sequential awaits) to compute 'missing'; for a large file this is O(chunks) round-trips and treats any get_blob error (network blip) as 'missing', re-uploading. No batched has-chunks control call.

defining_ts() does a full linear scan of the move log on every conflict-rename ranking, and readdir() calls it per colliding child; combined with is_trashed() walking ancestors per child, large directories with collisions are super-linear with no pathological-input bound beyond the edges.len() walk guard.

apply_remote_content re-folds the ENTIRE content map from scratch on every single remote op (fold_content sorts and replays the whole log). For a long-lived drive this is O(n^2) over the op stream, on the hot single-writer path the CLI/mount use.

VFS rename overwrite uses rm() then mv() as two separate MoveOps (not atomic across the pair) while the doc claims 'atomic by construction'. A crash/concurrent reader between the two ops sees the destination deleted but source not yet moved; if mv then fails the destination content is lost.

fresh_node_id relies on a process-global AtomicU64 that resets to 0 on restart; combined with a lamport-hex prefix, id uniqueness across restarts depends on the lamport clock advancing. Two fresh drives (lamport=0) on the same replica could mint colliding ids; a tree id collision corrupts identity, not just names.

No validation of file/dir names: empty names, names containing '/', '.'/'..', or NUL are accepted by mkdir/add_file in core (path-component sanitation exists only in the Windows winnames module, not enforced cross-platform). A name with '/' breaks path derivation/resolution.
robustnessIssues.location: ce-drive-core/src/drive.rs:172 mkdir, :185 add_file; tree.rs has no name validation

robustnessIssues.issue: DriveState is persisted as pretty JSON and the full move_log + content_log grows unbounded with every op (no compaction for the single-writer CLI path; checkpoint() exists only on the multi-writer SyncedDrive). A busy drive's state file and replay time grow without limit.
now_ms()/now_secs() swallow SystemTime errors to 0 (unwrap_or(0)). A clock set before epoch makes every mtime/expiry 0, silently breaking LWW ordering and expiry checks instead of erroring.

share.rs enforce_path_scope normalizes but only blocks literal '..' components; it does not reject backslashes or unicode-normalization tricks, and the scope check does not normalize case. On case-insensitive/NFD targets a scope bypass is conceivable (the mount has winnames for this, but the capability scope check does not use it).

testGaps.area: Live storage round-trip (put/get/delta against a real node) is only exercised by a node-gated test that skips when no node is present
Two-node multi-writer mesh convergence test is written but #[ignore]-gated and skips silently if the ce binary is absent — not in default CI
OS mount adapters (macOS FSKit, Windows WinFsp/ProjFS) FFI callback paths are never run; only winnames/projfs_logic are unit-tested
Large-file / OOM behavior and streaming bounds (no test feeds a file larger than a few chunks; the GB-file claim is untested)
VFS concurrency: simultaneous writers to the same handle, interleaved hydrate/flush, rename-during-write
Name validation rejection (empty, '/', '..', NUL, reserved) in core
wasm DriveCrdt JS surface (wasm-bindgen-test in deps but no actual browser/node wasm test present)
Trash restore / empty-trash / GC honoring retention (untested because unimplemented)
Crash-consistency of write_atomic + DriveState (interrupted save, partial JSON)
Capability scope bypass via case/unicode normalization and backslash separators

docGaps: README claims the repo 'currently ships the core (ce-drive-core) and a CLI — milestones M1 and M2' and lists ce-drive-mount/ce-drive-wasm under 'Not yet built', but both crates are fully implemented and shipped. The README is stale and understates the project.
No CHANGELOG and no per-crate README except a thin ce-drive-wasm one; ce-drive-mount and ce-drive-cli have only module docs.

No architecture/ADR doc in the repo's docs/ explaining the two-collection (tree vs content) orthogonality, Lamport+replica ordering, and merged-log convergence (design.md describes the vision but predates the shipped mount/wasm code).
No rustdoc doctests / runnable examples and no examples/ directory; the CLI usage in README is the only end-to-end example.
No documented file-size limits, streaming guarantees, or operational guidance (running a durable workspace, pin replication, recovering a corrupt state file).
The wasm JS API is documented in lib.rs rustdoc but no generated .d.ts is checked in nor a JS-side usage example/test harness.
